proposed_from.topic: sound-and-complete orbit-balance characterization for exact conformal cluster-effect prediction under finite matched-block randomization: for each known block permutation group and assignment support, prove equality of calibration- and target-selector mass on every member orbit iff selected scores are universally exchangeable over all bounded invariant block laws and equivariant learners; give a finite selector-feasibility LP and block-level rank-inversion algorithm, quantitative bounded undercoverage from every orbit gap, zero-coverage disjoint-orbit and paired-mate-leakage witnesses, the S2 matched-pair reduction, and the Shamba Maisha eight-pair facility consumer
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The core retains one target \\(T\\), its two coherent lifts, and one common \\(w\\). Observable selector laws are obtained by integrating legal realized-arm kernels over known randomization, avoiding incompatible target marginals.","published_axis":"The advance is not weighted conformal alone. It is the observable matched-randomization orbit quotient, its common-target unweighted LP certificate, and the sharp finite common-target orbit frontier."}
seed_list: Orbitwise equality of calibration and target member-arm selector masses is necessary and sufficient for universal selected-score exchangeability under finite matched-block randomization.
Exact calibration feasibility on an arbitrary finite assignment support is a selector-mixture transport problem with an LP certificate and rank inversion.
The worst conformal coverage loss from finite orbit imbalance has a sharp bounded-law frontier rather than a generic total-variation bound.
Within-pair mate calibration can have zero coverage because its member-arm orbit differs from the selected target orbit.
A local asymptotic efficiency bound might compare orbit-balanced weighted ranks with replay-based selective conformal prediction.
A selected-facility policy could minimize prediction-set width subject to orbit balance.
An \(S_2\) randomization limit law might quantify the width of exact pair-conformal intervals as the number of pairs grows.
Reference-set replay could be characterized as an orbit-balancing operator for arbitrary focal selectors.

seed_details.one_line: Orbitwise equality of calibration and target member-arm selector masses is necessary and sufficient for universal selected-score exchangeability under finite matched-block randomization.

anchor_paper.author: Wang, Li, and Yu
anchor_paper.claim: Finite-sample cluster-effect conformal prediction is developed under simple randomization.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS: the necessity direction requires an orbit-separating bounded law, not a restatement of rank validity.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from finite-group invariance because all selector weights are irrelevant within an orbit.
seed_details.rebuttal: The theorem compares two selector measures across distinct member-arm orbits and proves necessity by an adversarial invariant law.

seed_details.one_line: Exact calibration feasibility on an arbitrary finite assignment support is a selector-mixture transport problem with an LP certificate and rank inversion.

anchor_paper.author: Ramdas et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Permutation tests can use arbitrary distributions over arbitrary permutation subsets after randomization.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS: target matching, not arbitrary reference validity, is the new constraint.
seed_details.referee_objection: A simplex feasibility LP is routine.
seed_details.rebuttal: The substantive theorem is its equivalence to universal causal-score exchangeability and its conversion to effect prediction.

seed_details.one_line: The worst conformal coverage loss from finite orbit imbalance has a sharp bounded-law frontier rather than a generic total-variation bound.

anchor_paper.author: Barber et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Coverage loss is bounded under departures from exchangeability.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS: the named frontier is finite-orbit and requires attaining constructions.
seed_details.referee_objection: The answer is simply total variation.
seed_details.rebuttal: Finite rank discretization and two arm-specific inversions make the sharp constant and zero-coverage regime nontrivial.

seed_details.one_line: Within-pair mate calibration can have zero coverage because its member-arm orbit differs from the selected target orbit.

anchor_paper.author: Bai et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Matched-pair cluster inference has exact randomization validity for specified null hypotheses.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS: it is a corollary-sized reduction, not the main kernel.
seed_details.rebuttal: It remains a concrete adverse witness only when embedded in the member-arm selector characterization.

seed_details.one_line: A local asymptotic efficiency bound might compare orbit-balanced weighted ranks with replay-based selective conformal prediction.

anchor_paper.author: Jin and Ren
anchor_paper.claim: Replay reference sets yield exact selection-conditional coverage.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: FAIL: no identified smooth estimand or credible efficiency comparison is supplied.
seed_details.referee_objection: Conformal set efficiency has no canonical semiparametric estimand here.
seed_details.rebuttal: lands; dropped after the literature scan.

seed_details.one_line: A selected-facility policy could minimize prediction-set width subject to orbit balance.

anchor_paper.author: Koning and Hemerik
anchor_paper.claim: Representative subgroups improve exact group-invariance testing power.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: FAIL: width depends on unobserved outcomes and the policy objective lacks a certified observable criterion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an ungrounded design optimization.
seed_details.rebuttal: lands; dropped after the literature scan.

seed_details.one_line: An \(S_2\) randomization limit law might quantify the width of exact pair-conformal intervals as the number of pairs grows.

anchor_paper.author: Bai et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Matched-pair cluster ATE estimators have asymptotic inference theory.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: FAIL: it changes the finite-sample symmetry question into a generic asymptotic width problem.
seed_details.rebuttal: lands; dropped after the literature scan.

seed_details.one_line: Reference-set replay could be characterized as an orbit-balancing operator for arbitrary focal selectors.

anchor_paper.author: Jin and Ren
anchor_paper.claim: Permutation-invariant selection rules admit exact selection-conditional coverage through replay.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS: it would name a new operator, but it is less direct than the selector certificate.
seed_details.rebuttal: The closest paper does not express replay as a necessary orbit-mass condition on restricted randomization support.

proposed_from.literature_map: WangLiYu2026 is the direct causal-prediction anchor: it supplies finite-sample conformal treatment-effect intervals with arbitrary learners and names pair matching as future work. HemerikGoeman2018 supplies finite-group orbit logic, but for tests with one reference mechanism. RamdasBarberCandesTibshirani2023 removes uniform-group sampling for tests, not target-selector matching. KoningHemerik2024 studies economical exact subgroups, not selector transport. BarberCandesRamdasTibshirani2023 gives global nonexchangeability robustness, not a sharp finite-orbit converse. JinRen2025 addresses focal selection by replayed reference sets, not restricted matched-block supports. BaiLiuShaikhTabordMeehan2024 supplies the \(S_2\) matched-pair design and ATE inference. LeiCandes2021 is the counterfactual conformal ancestor. CohenEtAl2022 is the eight-pair facility consumer. The scan found no paper that states an iff orbit-mass certificate, an LP feasibility dual, or a member-arm mate-leakage result.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: All finite-coverage statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Coverage is conditional on iid replicated matched-block superpopulation arrays, independent external training, prespecified target selection, and auxiliary selector draws; it is not design-based coverage for the single Shamba Maisha finite population.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The unweighted and weighted coverage claims are accepted only in this replicated-array conditional regime.

banked_reason: Math review passed, but the single authorized D0.5 decision review found unresolved theorem-level comparator positioning and novelty below the field floor.
